item.title: Spark Cache Persist
item.author: seilylook
item.description: Introduction Spark Cache and Persist are optimization techniques for iterative and interactive Spark applications to improve the performances of the jobs or applications. In Key Point RDD.cache() cashes the RDD with the default storage level MEMORY_ONLY DataFrame.cache() caches the DataFrame with the default storage level MEMORY_AND_DISK The persist() method is used to store it to the user-defined storage level On Spark UI, the Storage tab shows where partitions exist in memory or disk across the cluster.

item.title: Spark UI
item.author: seilylook
item.description: Introduction Jobs: мҠӨнҢҢнҒ¬ м• н”ҢлҰ¬мјҖмқҙм…ҳмқҳ лӘЁл“  jobм—җ лҢҖн•ң мҡ”м•Ҫ м •ліҙ Stages: лӘЁл“  jobsмқҳ лӘЁл“  stagesмқҳ нҳ„мһ¬ мғҒнғң мҡ”м•Ҫ м •ліҙ Storage: persisted RDDмҷҖ DataFrame м •ліҙ м ңкіө Environment: лӢӨм–‘н•ң нҷҳкІҪ ліҖмҲҳ к°’ Executors: м• н”ҢлҰ¬мјҖмқҙм…ҳмқ„ мң„н•ҙ мғқм„ұлҗң Executer м •ліҙ м ңкіө. л©”лӘЁлҰ¬мҷҖ л””мҠӨнҒ¬ мӮ¬мҡ©лҹүкіј task, shuffle м •ліҙ л“ұ SQL: м• н”ҢлҰ¬мјҖмқҙм…ҳмқҙ Spark SQL мҝјлҰ¬ мӢӨн–ү мӢң м •ліҙ м ңкіө Streaming: Streaming jobs мӢӨн–ү мӢң м •ліҙ м ңкіө м• н”ҢлҰ¬мјҖмқҙм…ҳ мӢӨн–ү 1 2 3 4 5 df = spark.read.format("csv") \ .

item.title: Spark Execution Plan
item.author: seilylook
item.description: Execution plan goal An execution plan is the set of operations executed to translate a query language statement (SQL, Spark SQL, Dataframe operations etc.) to a set of optimized logical and physical operations. Execution plan is a set of operations that will be executed from the SQL(or Spark SQL) statement to the DAG which will be send to Spark Executors. Info DAG? Directed Acyclic Graph. A DAG is an acyclic graph produced by the DAG scheduler in Spark.

item.title: Spark Hierarchy
item.author: seilylook
item.description: Introduction Spark’s execution hierarchy, from top to bottom is Job, Stage, Task. Slots are part of executors. A task is executed in a slot. But slots are rather a tool to execute tasks, not part of the execution hierarchy itself. Executors are a component of a Spark cluter, but not of the execution hierarchy. Hardware Hierarchy Cluster Driver Executor Cores / Slots: Each executor can be considered as servers and they have cores.

item.title: Spark Dependency
item.author: seilylook
item.description: Introduction Transformations are operations on RDDs, Dataframes, or Dataset, that produce new RDDs, Dataframes, or Datasets. Transformations are lazy evaluated, which means they are not executed until an action is called. Spark uses transformation to build a DAG(Directed Acyclic Graph) of computation that represents the sequence of steps required to compute the final result. Transformations can be categorized as either Narrow or Wide based on whether their dependency on input data partitions.

item.title: Spark DPP(Dynamic Partition Pruning)
item.author: seilylook
item.description: Partition Pruning in Spark In standard database pruning means that the optimizer will avoid reading files that cannot contain the data that you are looking for. For example, 1 2 3 SELECT * FROM students WHERE subject = 'English'; In this simple query, we are trying to match and identify records in the Students table that belong to subject English. This translates into a simple form that is a filter on top of a scan which means whole data gets scanned first and then filtered out according to the condition.

item.title: Spark AQE(Adaptive Query Execution)
item.author: seilylook
item.description: Info PartitionмқҖ RDDsлӮҳ DatasetлҘј кө¬м„ұн•ҳкі  мһҲлҠ” мөңмҶҢ лӢЁмң„ к°қмІҙмһ…лӢҲлӢӨ. к°Ғ PartitionмқҖ м„ңлЎң лӢӨлҘё л…ёл“ңм—җм„ң л¶„мӮ° мІҳлҰ¬лҗ©лӢҲлӢӨ. Sparkм—җм„ңлҠ” н•ҳлӮҳмқҳ мөңмҶҢ м—°мӮ°мқ„ Taskлқјкі  н‘ңнҳ„н•ҳлҠ”лҚ°, мқҙ н•ҳлӮҳмқҳ Taskм—җм„ң н•ҳлӮҳмқҳ Partitionмқҙ мІҳлҰ¬лҗ©лӢҲлӢӨ. лҳҗн•ң, н•ҳлӮҳмқҳ TaskлҠ” н•ҳлӮҳмқҳ Coreк°Җ м—°мӮ° мІҳлҰ¬н•©лӢҲлӢӨ. мҰү, 1 Core = 1 Task = 1 Partitionмһ…лӢҲлӢӨ. м„Өм •лҗң Partition мҲҳм—җ л”°лқј к°Ғ Partitionмқҳ нҒ¬кё°к°Җ кІ°м •лҗ©лӢҲлӢӨ. к·ёлҰ¬кі  мқҙ Partitionмқҳ нҒ¬кё°к°Җ кІ°көӯ Core лӢ№ н•„мҡ”н•ң л©”лӘЁлҰ¬ нҒ¬кё°лҘј кІ°м •н•ҳкІҢ лҗ©лӢҲлӢӨ. в—јпёҸ Partition мҲҳ вҶ’ Core мҲҳ

item.title: Spark Architecture
item.author: seilylook
item.description: Terms Term Meainig Application User program built on Spark. Consists of a driver program and executors on the cluster. Application jar A jar containing the user’s Spark application. In some cases users will want to create an “uber jar” containing their application along with its dependencies. The user’s jar should never include Hadoop or Spark libraries, however, these will be added at runtime. Driver program The process running the main() function of the application and creating the SparkContext Cluster manager An external service for acquiring resources on the cluster (e.

item.title: Spark Partition
item.author: seilylook
item.description: What is Partition? PartitionмқҖ RDDsлӮҳ Datasetмқ„ кө¬м„ұн•ҳкі  мһҲлҠ” мөңмҶҢ лӢЁмң„ к°қмІҙмқҙлӢӨ. к°Ғ PartitionмқҖ м„ңлЎң лӢӨлҘё л…ёл“ңм—җм„ң л¶„мӮ° мІҳлҰ¬лҗңлӢӨ. Sparkм—җм„ңлҠ” н•ҳлӮҳмқҳ мөңмҶҢ м—°мӮ°мқ„ Taskлқјкі  н‘ңнҳ„н•ҳлҠ”лҚ°, мқҙ н•ҳлӮҳмқҳ Taskм—җм„ң н•ҳлӮҳмқҳ Partitionмқҙ мІҳлҰ¬лҗңлӢӨ. лҳҗн•ң, н•ҳлӮҳмқҳ TaskлҠ” н•ҳлӮҳмқҳ Coreк°Җ м—°мӮ° мІҳлҰ¬н•ңлӢӨ. мҰү, 1 Core = 1 Task = 1 PartitionмқҙлӢӨ. мҳҲлҘј л“Өм–ҙ, лӢӨмқҢкіј к°ҷлӢӨл©ҙ м „мІҙ Core мҲҳлҘј 300к°ңлЎң м„ёнҢ…н•ң мғҒнғңмқҙкі , мқҙ 300к°ңк°Җ нҳ„мһ¬ мӢӨн–ү мӨ‘мқё Task мҲҳмқҙмһҗ, нҳ„мһ¬ мІҳлҰ¬ мӨ‘мқё Partition мҲҳм—җ н•ҙлӢ№н•ңлӢӨ. лҳҗн•ң, м „мІҙ Partition мҲҳлҠ” 1800к°ңлЎң м„ёнҢ…н–Ҳмңјл©°, мқҙлҠ” м „мІҙ Task мҲҳмқҙкё°лҸ„ н•ҳлӢӨ.

item.title: SQL Join
item.author: seilylook
item.description: Join? JOINмқҖ лҚ°мқҙн„°лІ мқҙмҠӨ лӮҙмқҳ м—¬лҹ¬ н…Ңмқҙлё”м—җм„ң к°Җм ёмҳЁ л ҲмҪ”л“ңлҘј мЎ°н•©н•ҳм—¬ н•ҳлӮҳмқҳ н…Ңмқҙлё”мқҙлӮҳ кІ°кіј м§‘н•©мңјлЎң н‘ңнҳ„н•ҙ мЈјлҠ” кІғмқҙлӢӨ. Inner join: көҗм§‘н•© Left / Right join: л¶Җл¶„ м§‘н•© Outer Join: н•©м§‘н•© мҳҲмӢң лҚ°мқҙн„° ID ENAME 1 AAA 2 BBB 3 CCC ID KNAME 1 к°Җ 2 лӮҳ 4 лқј 5 л§Ҳ Inner Join мЎ°мқён•ҳлҠ” н…Ңмқҙлё”мқҳ ON м Ҳмқҳ мЎ°кұҙмқј мқјм№ҳн•ҳлҠ” кІ°кіјл§Ң м¶ңл Ҙ 1 2 3 4 select u.userid, name from usertbl as u inner join buytbl as b on u.
